title: Fixing Missing Right Child Node Handling in a Binary Tree

statement.markdown: Given a binary tree node class and a method `insertNode` that inserts a new node at the correct position, modify the code to correctly handle the right child node.
statement.inputFormat: Root node of a binary tree (null or a valid TreeNode)
statement.outputFormat: Root node of the modified binary tree

examples.explanation: Inserts a node with value 4 as the right child of 3.

hints.text: Review the logic for inserting a node as the right child.

editorial.title: Correctly Handling Missing Right Child Node in a Binary Tree
editorial.intuition: The correct approach involves using a recursive or iterative method to traverse the tree and insert the new node at the correct position.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would involve creating a new tree with each insertion and then returning the root node.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves modifying the existing tree node class to correctly handle the insertion of new nodes as the right child.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The code is correct because it properly handles the insertion of new nodes as the right child, ensuring the tree remains valid.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Insert node 4 as the right child of 3, ensuring the tree remains valid.
commonMistakes: Incorrectly handling the right child node.
Not updating the tree structure correctly.
